key_insight: A Lagrange-multiplier correction can enforce area-decaying, structure-preserving mean curvature flow while retaining optimal finite-element accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This gives a stable, high-order moving-surface FEM framework for geometry evolution problems where preserving physical dissipation and mesh quality matters.
method: Evolving surface FEM based on Huisken-style equations for the normal vector and mean curvature.
Fully discrete time stepping with linearly implicit BDF for geometric variables and implicit Adams updates for nodal positions.
Optional minimal-deformation-rate tangential velocities plus a discrete Lagrange multiplier solved by simplified Newton iteration.
evidence_signal: The paper proves local well-posedness and optimal error bounds of order h^k + τ^q, and numerical tests on sphere mean-curvature flow match the predicted convergence rates with only small overhead.
watch_item: The strongest error guarantees rely on higher regularity assumptions, so performance may depend on smoothness of the evolving surface and robust nonlinear solves.
paper_title: Structure-preserving Lagrange-multiplier methods for mean curvature flow and their error bounds
